key_insight: Two-Sided L-BFGS adds a two-sided geometric safeguard that keeps the inverse Hessian approximation uniformly well-conditioned while preserving the efficiency of standard L-BFGS.
engineering_relevance: This improves the stability and robustness of large-scale optimization in ill-conditioned settings without changing the usual O(mn) memory and per-iteration cost.
method: Bound the inverse Hessian condition number with a geometric envelope.
Track extreme eigenvalues across m quasi-Newton updates from a scaled identity start.
Retain standard L-BFGS curvature accumulation under strong Wolfe line search assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on high-dimensional optimization problems showing better-conditioned inverse Hessian approximations and improved robustness on ill-conditioned benchmarks.
watch_item: The safeguard adds envelope hyperparameters and relies on strong Wolfe line search assumptions, so practical tuning and line-search compliance may affect performance.
paper_title: On the Condition Number Upper Bound of the L-BFGS Inverse Hessian Approximation Matrix with a Two-Sided Geometric Envelope Safeguarding Mechanism
